{
  "pages": [
    {
      "slug": "summary-archiving-claude-conv-to-obsidian-vault",
      "page_type": "summary",
      "title": "Archiving Claude Conv to Obsidian Vault (summary)",
      "body": "This guide provides a structured workflow and Python script to parse local Claude session data, convert it to Obsidian markdown files, and integrate those files into a searchable knowledge base. The process covers reading the [[claude-jsonl-format|JSONL structure]], automating the conversion using a [[claude-to-obsidian-pipeline|Python parsing script and cron job]], and applying [[obsidian-knowledge-retrieval|retrieval patterns]] such as Dataview queries and Maps of Content (MOCs)."
    },
    {
      "slug": "claude-jsonl-format",
      "page_type": "concept",
      "title": "Claude JSONL Format",
      "body": "Claude local session data is stored as line-delimited JSON (JSONL). Each line represents a discrete turn containing a `timestamp` and a `message` object with a designated `role` ('user' or 'assistant'). The `content` value of the message can be a raw string or an array of blocks, which requires parsing block types (such as 'text') to reconstruct the conversation text."
    },
    {
      "slug": "claude-to-obsidian-pipeline",
      "page_type": "concept",
      "title": "Claude to Obsidian Pipeline",
      "body": "A pipeline for archiving conversation history reads local `.jsonl` session files, extracts timestamps and text blocks from the [[claude-jsonl-format|Claude JSONL format]], and converts them to frontmatter-enriched markdown files inside an Obsidian vault. Automation is achieved by scheduling the Python script as a background service via a utility like cron to ensure nightly, incremental synchronization of new and modified sessions."
    },
    {
      "slug": "obsidian-knowledge-retrieval",
      "page_type": "concept",
      "title": "Obsidian Knowledge Retrieval",
      "body": "Once external content is synchronized to a vault via an automated parser like the [[claude-to-obsidian-pipeline|Claude to Obsidian pipeline]], it can be synthesized into a personal knowledge base using structured retrieval patterns. These include automatic tag-based organization, dynamic Dataview database queries to aggregate documents by date and project metadata, and manually curated Map of Content (MOC) index notes to link related research threads."
    }
  ]
}
}
